source: CEFR B1 descriptors + standard reference French grammar (Grammaire progressive du français, CLE International)

cards.title: Full narrative
cards.goal: Can tell a story or describe an experience as a connected, structured narrative with background, events and a point.
functions: décrire des expériences et des événements
grammar: passé composé + imparfait woven together (foreground vs background)
past-tense agreement with être verbs and preceding direct objects
discourse: sequencers: d'abord, ensuite, puis, plus tard, finalement
cards.evidence: Tell a 2–3 minute story with a clear beginning, complication and ending, correctly splitting foreground (passé composé) from background (imparfait); listener retells the main events in order.
skin_examples: how you met a close friend

cards.title: Before the past
cards.goal: Can make the order of past events explicit when narrating, including events that happened earlier.
cards.evidence: Retell a story that needs at least three plus-que-parfait clauses to make the sequence clear.
skin_examples: why a plan fell through (what had already happened)

cards.title: Past habits
cards.goal: Can describe how life used to be, contrasting repeated past habits with the present.
cards.evidence: 90-second 'when I was younger' monologue using imparfait for at least four distinct past habits or states, contrasted explicitly with maintenant + present tense.
cards.note: French uses one form (imparfait) for both English 'used to' and 'would' — no split to teach, but the present-tense contrast must be explicit or the habitual sense gets lost.

cards.title: Finished vs unfinished time
cards.goal: Can say how long a situation has been going on and distinguish it from a completed past event.
grammar: depuis + present tense for ongoing situations
il y a … que / ça fait … que
depuis vs il y a (ongoing vs completed point in time)
cards.evidence: Answer five how-long questions about real situations, using depuis + present for the three still true and il y a + passé composé for the two finished.
skin_examples: how long you've been learning something
cards.note: The trap: English 'I have lived here for 3 years' maps to French present tense (j'habite ici depuis 3 ans), not a compound past — this is the single most common fossilised error at this level.

cards.title: The very recent past
cards.goal: Can say that something has just happened.
cards.evidence: Produce five true venir de sentences about things that just happened (today, this hour), including one in the imparfait (venait de) for a past-in-the-past reading.
skin_examples: a narrative moment interrupted by something that had just happened

cards.title: Reported speech
cards.goal: Can report what someone said or asked.
grammar: discours indirect: present → imparfait, passé composé → plus-que-parfait, futur → conditionnel
reported questions: si, ce qui/ce que, interrogative word retained
cards.evidence: Report a short dialogue to a third person: two statements, one yes/no question and one wh-question, with correct tense backshift and si/interrogative-word choice.

cards.title: Simple passive
cards.goal: Can describe processes, events and general facts where the agent is unknown or unimportant.
grammar: voix passive, présent et passé composé
past-participle agreement with the passive subject
cards.evidence: Describe a process or report an event in six sentences, at least four passive with correct participle agreement, defending one active/passive choice when asked.

cards.title: Opinions with reasons
cards.goal: Can express personal views and opinions on familiar topics and support them.
discourse: fuller justification beyond parce que: c'est pour ça que, du coup
exponents: Je ne pense pas que… (+ subjonctif)
cards.evidence: One-minute opinion on a familiar topic with two distinct supporting reasons and one example, correctly switching to the subjunctive after a negated opinion if used.

cards.title: Agreeing & disagreeing politely
cards.goal: Can agree and disagree with others, including softened disagreement, and hold his/her position in informal discussion.
exponents: Je vois ce que tu veux dire, mais…
Je ne suis pas vraiment d'accord
cards.evidence: Ten-statement reaction round using at least six different exponents, including two softened disagreements; defend one disagreement for 30 seconds.

cards.title: Reviewing & recommending
cards.goal: Can summarise a film, book, show or piece of media and give an opinion about it with a recommendation.
functions: parler de films et de livres
exponents: Si tu aimes X, tu vas aimer ça
cards.evidence: 90-second spoken review (what it is, what it's about, what worked, who should watch/read it), then answer two detail questions.

cards.title: Linking ideas: cause, effect, contrast
cards.goal: Can connect a series of points into linear, linked discourse using explicit logical markers.
cards.evidence: Present both sides of a familiar dilemma in one minute using d'un-côté/de-l'autre-côté plus two other logical markers, concluding with donc.
skin_examples: working from home vs the office

cards.title: Imagining differently (present/future)
cards.goal: Can talk about hypothetical or unlikely present and future situations.
cards.evidence: Answer three what-would-you-do-if questions with full si-clause sentences, then pose two of your own.
skin_examples: if you had an extra day off

cards.title: Regrets & alternate pasts
cards.goal: Can speculate about how past events could have been different.
grammar: j'aurais dû / je n'aurais pas dû
cards.evidence: Retell a real past decision and give two conditionnel-passé alternatives, plus one aurais-dû judgment.
skin_examples: a trip that could have gone differently

cards.title: Deduction about the present
cards.goal: Can draw logical conclusions about present situations from evidence.
grammar: devoir / ça doit être for deduction
ça ne peut pas être for near-certainty against
contrast with il se peut que + subjonctif for weaker certainty
cards.evidence: Given five clues, produce deductions graded correctly across ça doit être / il se peut que / ça ne peut pas être.

cards.title: Judging & speculating about the past
cards.goal: Can criticise past actions and speculate about what may have happened.
grammar: devoir/pouvoir + infinitif passé: il a dû partir, il n'a pas pu venir
aurait dû / n'aurait pas dû for criticism (consolidating FR-B1-13)
cards.evidence: React to three what-went-wrong scenarios with one aurait-dû judgment and one devoir/pouvoir-past speculation each.

cards.title: Opening & closing conversations
cards.goal: Can initiate a conversation naturally and end it politely.
exponents: Excusez-moi de vous déranger, je me demandais si…
Tu ne devineras jamais ce qui m'est arrivé…
Ça m'a fait plaisir de discuter avec toi
Bon, je dois y aller — à bientôt
cards.evidence: Three cold-start roleplays (stranger, acquaintance, service situation): open, sustain for one minute, close appropriately.
skin_examples: meeting someone new at an event

cards.title: Keeping the conversation alive
cards.goal: Can interrupt politely, change the topic, return to a dropped topic, and keep a conversation flowing with natural spoken markers.
functions: gérer l'interaction: interrompre, changer de sujet, reprendre
discourse: informal markers: ben, alors, du coup, enfin, quoi
cards.evidence: Five-minute genuine conversation in which the student must interrupt once, change topic once and steer back once, reacting with at least four informal markers (teacher tallies).
skin_examples: any free conversation — skins itself with the week's topic

cards.title: Checking & clarifying
cards.goal: Can check understanding in both directions and repair breakdowns without abandoning the message.
exponents: Je ne suis pas sûr(e) de comprendre — tu es en train de dire que… ?
cards.evidence: Deliberate-obstacle task: reach full understanding of a slightly-too-complex explanation using at least three different clarification moves, then explain it back.

cards.title: Problems & non-routine situations
cards.goal: Can explain why something is a problem and deal with less routine situations (complaints, changes, obstacles) in shops, travel and services.
grammar: il faut que + subjonctif présent (regular forms + être, avoir, aller, faire, pouvoir, vouloir)
lexis: travel and services: enregistrement, salle d'embarquement, carte bancaire
cards.evidence: Two escalation roleplays (wrong booking; faulty purchase): state the problem, explain why it matters using il faut que at least once, propose a resolution, respond to pushback.

cards.title: Explaining how
cards.goal: Can describe how to do something, giving detailed step-by-step instructions.
discourse: instructional sequencing: d'abord, ensuite, une fois que…, assurez-vous de…
cards.evidence: Teach the listener a real skill in 2–3 minutes, using at least one pour-que purpose clause; success measured by whether the listener could actually do it.
skin_examples: how to use an app or tool

cards.title: Refining the future
cards.goal: Can make predictions with graded confidence and describe plans for the future.
cards.evidence: This-time-next-year monologue: three graded predictions (certain/probable/possible, each marked linguistically) plus two quand-clause sentences.
skin_examples: where you'll be in a year
how a project will turn out
cards.note: English uses present tense after 'when' for future time (when I arrive…); French requires futur (quand j'arriverai…) — a persistent transfer error.
